scans.name: private external-reference trace scan
scans.command: rg -n forbidden external-reference terms wild_boar_proxy tests audit_results/ui_web_global_canonical_surface_audit_pass_2026-05-15

scans.name: release and desktop claim scan
scans.command: rg -n fake release/desktop claims wild_boar_proxy/web_design_ui tests/test_web_design_ui.py
scans.classification: Matches were negative assertions in tests only. UI markup and runtime JavaScript had no forbidden release or desktop readiness claim.

scans.name: browser input and raw read scan
scans.classification: UI hits are details elements, CSS selectors, safe redaction labels, and preview-only source/state selects. No file picker, editable path, token, secret, or raw config editor is active in the audited surfaces.

scans.name: raw config and JSON editor scan
scans.classification: UI markup and runtime JavaScript do not expose editable raw config surfaces. Test hits are negative assertions or redaction fixtures. Route secret_ref appears only as a reference label, not a secret value.

scans.name: visible icon scan
scans.command: Chrome DevTools Protocol DOM metrics at 1600x1000
scans.classification: visibleSvgIcons=0 on audited routes; visibleImgIcons were present.

truth_boundary.command_ok_is_not_runtime_truth: Covered by existing action ledger and onboarding tests; no runtime or command surface changed in this contour.
truth_boundary.stale_green: State matrix shows stale banners on primary planned routes. Some green sub-statuses remain on diagnostics/setup where they describe component-level admitted/OK facts, not global runtime health.
